rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. KAO VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 14, 2010, line 3. DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MARTHA PEUGH-WADE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied, summary adjudication is denied. As to the 1st, 2nd, and 5th causes of action, plaintiff raises triable issues of fact whether U.S.F. was really concerned about plaintiff's fitness to perform his job, and whether the examination was sought because of businees needs. As to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, a triable issue of fact is raised as to what information was being requested by U.S.F. The letters to plaintiff and to Dr. Reynolds, on their face, arguably request more information than what is allowed. As to the 6th cause of action, a triable issue of fact is raised whether Ms. Peugh-Wade acted with malice, or without reasonable belief in the truth of the statements made. =(302/CWW)
